title: Power and Machines Laboratory

Power and Machines Laboratory

Fakülte

The students who will graduate from our Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering are required to gain competent and adequate equipment to have an active role in the research of problems and solution methods encountered in Energy, Power Systems and Electrical Machines applications in Industry. For this purpose, it is important to assimilate Power Systems and Electrical Machines courses and laboratory applications among the courses they study. In addition, it is important for our graduates to carry out advanced projects in the field of Electrical and Electronics Engineering in the institutions where they will work, and to be trained for feedback to our university. For this reason, it is aimed for the students who will graduate to make projects in the Power Systems and Electrical Machines Laboratory focused on problems for the industry. Some of the main topics are; Reactive power compensation, Modeling for losses of transmission lines, Investigation of transients in transmission lines, Investigation of voltage fluctuations and losses in transmission lines, Investigation of problems arising in solar and wind energy, Investigation of control circuits for overload protection, Motor control drives, PV systems, Rectifiers, AC and DC Choppers, Converters, Inverters.

In addition, thanks to the "Power Systems and Electrical Machines Laboratory", students will gain practice in the maintenance, operation and design of power systems, electrical machines and renewable energy systems. They will also use this laboratory in their graduation projects. The laboratory will also provide graduate students with an environment where they can implement advanced projects such as motor controllers, solar energy applications and power electronics applications, and provide them with the opportunity to further their professional experience.
